meta.title: Jalapão Gaviões do Cerrado — Adventure itineraries in Tocantins, Brazil
meta.description: Specialized travel boutique for Jalapão, Chapada das Mesas, and Serra Gerais. Human support on WhatsApp, well-paced wilderness trips rooted in Brazil’s cerrado biome.
meta.keywords: Jalapão, Tocantins, Chapada das Mesas, Serra Gerais, eco travel, Jalapão Gaviões do Cerrado, Brazil safari

hero.title: Experience Jalapão and Tocantins with guides shaped by road and wilderness.
hero.subtitle: Carefully crafted routes, safety first, sincere hospitality—from first WhatsApp ping to farewell.
hero.imageAlt: Jalapão river bordered by cerrado vegetation in natural daylight, green-tinted water

about.title: About us
about.lead: We chase wide silences, gold-hour light over savanna grasses, and the pulse of northern Brazil.
about.p1: Jalapão Gaviões do Cerrado curates itineraries that honour each landscape’s pacing, respecting communities and ecosystems while staying close via WhatsApp from planning to postcards home.
about.p2: We serve Jalapão, Chapada das Mesas, and Serra Gerais routes—regions that reinvent themselves with rainy and dry spells.

bullets.b1: Comfortable pacing for families and intimate groups
bullets.b2: Iconic must-sees plus local curveballs worth the detours
bullets.b3: Seasonal logistics, packing lists, and honest expectations

about.cadasturNote: We will showcase the official CADASTUR certificate from Brazil’s tourism ministry once the compliant asset arrives.

routesSection.title: Itineraries
routesSection.lead: Pick your highlight—we’ll already know why you knocked when WhatsApp pings.
routesSection.itineraryHeading: Itinerary in text — open each block when ready

jalapao.title: Jalapão
jalapao.description: State park staples—fervedouros, dunes, Cachoeira da Velha—with breathing room baked into every day.
jalapao.altCover: Jalapão river winding through cerrado greenery
jalapao.itineraryIntro: Illustrative outline—we lock the real order, times, and lodges with you on WhatsApp.

panels.title: Day 1 — Arrival
panels.body:
Pickup at the agreed hub (airport, hotel, or roadside meet point).
Scenic transfers with short stretch breaks.
Check-in and light briefing on pacing, hydration, sunscreen, and what sunrise looks like tomorrow.

panels.title: Day 2 — Springs & riverbanks
panels.body:
Slow introduction to fervedouros and river beaches.
Wide windows for swims and portraits.
Lunch breaks with locals when logistics allow before an early evening rest.

panels.title: Day 3 — Dunes & lookouts
panels.body:
Optional dune crossings or mellow ridge hikes.
Quiet golden-hour moments and campfire talks when skies cooperate.

panels.title: Day 4 — Signature falls & farewell
panels.body:
Big-water viewpoints (e.g. Cachoeira da Velha on your itinerary build), long pauses.
Departures synced with onward flights via WhatsApp confirmations.

chapada-mesas.title: Chapada das Mesas
chapada-mesas.description: Amazon-inflected plateaus begging for wide-angle lenses—long days mirrored by luminous sunsets.
chapada-mesas.altCover: Chapada das Mesas open horizon with plateau vegetation
chapada-mesas.itineraryIntro: Model text—daily flow shifts with seasons and group size.

panels.title: Day 1 — Settling into the plateau
panels.body:
Arrival hospitality and lodging near key lookouts.
Short neighborhood walk or sunset vantage to acclimate to heat and altitude.

panels.title: Day 2 — Canyons & mesa horizons
panels.body:
Panoramic belvederes explaining how the sandstone mesas evolved.
Plenty of water breaks and shaded pauses.
Flexible timing for drone-free photo sessions.

panels.title: Day 3 — Waterfalls & swims
panels.body:
Full swim blocks at cascading pools.
Optional short walks to quieter grottoes when stamina matches.

panels.title: Days 4–5 — Communities & exit
panels.body:
Considerate visits to artisan partners.
Comfort food stops on the highway back and luggage checks before outbound transfers.

serra-gerais.title: Serra Gerais
serra-gerais.description: Vibrant communities, braided rivers, trekking depth—classic cerrado energy with hospitable pacing.
serra-gerais.altCover: Rolling cerrado ridges in Serra Gerais sunlight
serra-gerais.itineraryIntro: Flexible circuit around rivers, trails, and local stories.

panels.title: Day 1 — Entering the cerrado maze
panels.body:
Transfer with interpretive roadside stops covering buriti corridors and river basins.
Low-volume travel etiquette chat over dinner.
Sunrise pact for hikers who crave early light.

panels.title: Day 2 — Trails & braided rivers
panels.body:
Moderate trekking paced to the slowest teammate.
Swimholes shaded by palms or sandstone bands.
Hammock naps welcomed when the casa base offers them.

panels.title: Day 3 — Culture & farewell
panels.body:
Short loops through partner communities.
Farewell meal and transfer timing matched to onward connections.

testimonials.title: Guest voices
testimonials.lead: What travelers replay most: fewer rushed checklists, more connection to place.

items.name: Marina Costa
items.quote: Perfect rhythm—time to float in fervedouros, chase dawn light, and unwind at night. Quick answers on WhatsApp every step.

items.name: Ricardo & Paula
items.quote: We traveled with kids and felt supported from planning to our last Chapada stop. Logistics never felt stressful.

items.name: Anete Souza
items.quote: An honest pick for anyone tired of cookie-cutter packages. We’re already plotting the Serra Gerais return.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: Share travel dates, party size, and dream destination—we reply fast on WhatsApp.
contact.location: Tocantins, Brazil · Jalapão, Chapada das Mesas & Serra Gerais
contact.midPageSubtitle: One WhatsApp note is enough to kick things off.

ctaBanner.title: Ready to map your next adventure?
ctaBanner.subtitle: Open a chat and we’ll sketch a proposal around your calendar.

whatsappMessages.jalapao: Hi! I’m interested in a Jalapão itinerary—can we talk?
whatsappMessages.chapada-mesas: Hello! I’d love pricing for Chapada das Mesas.
whatsappMessages.serra-gerais: Hi! May I get details on Serra Gerais routes?

whatsappMessages.afterRoutes: Hi! I saw your itineraries online and have a few quick questions.
